question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: protobufHttpMessageConverter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a protobuf body with deeply nested message fields that cause mergeFrom() to trigger recursive parsing, leading to a stack overflow or OOM that crashes the gossip-prevention processing thread and allows subsequent requests to bypass transaction validation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: etagFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a GET request to /api/v1/network/fees with a crafted If-None-Match header to probe whether a specific fee schedule (corresponding to a gossip-triggering transaction type) is cached, using the 304 response as a side-channel to infer which transaction types are currently active on the network?
